{
    "thoughts": [
        "The user wants me to run a GChat sync task based on the current day of the week (Saturday, March 28, 2026).",
        "According to the rules: Tuesday-Saturday = DAYS=2, Sunday = DAYS=3, Monday = DAYS=4.",
        "Today is Saturday, so DAYS=2.",
        "I need to run the gchat_reader.py script with --days 2.",
        "The script requires Chrome DevTools at 192.168.1.11:9223 - I should verify this connection first or just proceed assuming it's ready.",
        "After running, I need to monitor the task up to 10 times with 30-second intervals by tailing the debug log.",
        "On completion, send a success notification; on failure, send an error notification."
    ],
    "headline": "Executing GChat sync task for Saturday (DAYS=2)",
    "tool_name": "code_execution_tool",
    "tool_args": {
        "runtime": "terminal",
        "session": 0,
        "reset": false,
        "code": "python /a0/usr/skills/gchat/scripts/gchat_reader.py --days 2"
    }
}

thoughts: The user wants me to run a GChat sync task based on the current day of the week (Saturday, March 28, 2026).
According to the rules: Tuesday-Saturday = DAYS=2, Sunday = DAYS=3, Monday = DAYS=4.
Today is Saturday, so DAYS=2.
I need to run the gchat_reader.py script with --days 2.
The script requires Chrome DevTools at 192.168.1.11:9223 - I should verify this connection first or just proceed assuming it's ready.
After running, I need to monitor the task up to 10 times with 30-second intervals by tailing the debug log.
On completion, send a success notification; on failure, send an error notification.
kvps.headline: Executing GChat sync task for Saturday (DAYS=2)
